references.source: NHS digital service manual accessibility content guidance
references.borrowed: Clear labels, visible status copy, and validation language that does not rely on color alone.
references.rejected: No NHS-branded component chrome because the clinical workspace owns local styling.

references.borrowed: Diff and reason details use native disclosure buttons with aria-expanded and aria-controls.
Multiple coded reasons use labelled native checkboxes grouped by a visible fieldset legend.
references.rejected: No custom checkbox widget or tri-state behavior.

references.borrowed: Used to avoid modal semantics because the inline rail sheet does not make the rest of the shell inert.
references.rejected: No modal dialog or focus trap for the rail reason sheet.

references.borrowed: Locator-scoped ARIA baselines for compact and reason-open override states.
references.rejected: No full-page ARIA baseline because unrelated staff shell chrome is outside task scope.

references.borrowed: Each fixture runs in an isolated browser context.
references.rejected: No shared route state between override fixtures.

references.borrowed: Element-level screenshot baselines for compact, mandatory reason, and completed trail states.
Traces with screenshots and snapshots are captured for fixture debugging.
Dense work surfaces should keep keyboard traversal predictable and low-noise.
references.rejected: No global shortcut layer for override capture.

references.borrowed: Trail rows use compact actor, timestamp, and status metadata rather than explanatory prose.
Action trail and chain refs use dense structured rows for scan speed.
Visible labels, helper text, and validation message placement for reason capture.
references.rejected: No two-column form inside the rail because width is constrained to 320px.

references.borrowed: Approval burden notice uses compact status copy near the related action.
references.rejected: No dismissible notification because the reason requirement cannot be hidden.
